page.content: The "Adjusted All Residences" shows results for 0 years with a Spana of 0.90, cases/controls of 95/1382, a deviance p-value of 0.36, and figure 3a, while at 15 years, the Spana is 0.30, cases/controls are 45/665, and the deviance p-value is 0.05 with figure 3b. In contrast, the "Crude All Residences" at 15 years shows a Spana of 0.40, cases/controls of 45/665, and a deviance p-value of 0.11 with no figure, while another entry at 15 years indicates a Spana of 0.30b, cases/controls of 45/665, and no deviance p-value with figure 3c. The "Adjusted Longest Duration Residence" indicates 15 years with a Spana of 0.30b, cases/controls of 29/417, and no deviance p-value with figure 3d, while at 15 years, it shows a Spana of 0.95, cases/controls of 29/417, and a deviance p-value of 0.41 with no figure.

page.content: Regarding demographics, healthy controls consist of 119 individuals with a gender split of 53/66, aged 46.3 ± 10.2 years, an education rating of 4.0 ± 1.3, and no CAG data, while premanifest includes 120 individuals with a gender split of 54/66, aged 40.8 ± 8.8 years, an education rating of 3.94 ± 1.2, and CAG at 43.1 ± 2.4, and manifest includes 118 individuals with a gender split of 54/64, aged 48.5 ± 9.9 years, an education rating of 3.65 ± 1.3, and CAG at 43.7 ± 3.0; total intracranial volumes are 1392 ± 136 mL for healthy controls, 1408 ± 151 mL for premanifest, and 1362 ± 130 mL for manifest.
